title: cxxrtl: eliding wires connected to a $memwr impacts model behaviour
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

RTLIL:
```
module \top
  wire width 2 input 0 \w_addr
  wire width 1 input 1 \w_en
  wire width 8 input 2 \w_data
  wire width 1 input 3 \clk
  wire width 1 input 4 \rst
  memory width 8 size 4 \mem
  cell $meminit $1
    parameter \MEMID "\\mem"
    parameter \ABITS 2'11
    parameter \WIDTH 4'1000
    parameter \WORDS 3'100
    parameter \PRIORITY 1'0
    connect \ADDR 3'000
    connect \DATA 32'00000000000000000000000000000000
  end
  wire width 1 \mem_w_en
  wire width 2 \mem_w_addr
  wire width 8 \mem_w_data
  cell $memwr \wp
    parameter \MEMID "\\mem"
    parameter \ABITS 2'10
    parameter \WIDTH 4'1000
    parameter \CLK_ENABLE 1'1
    parameter \CLK_POLARITY 1'1
    parameter \PRIORITY 1'0
    connect \CLK \clk
    connect \EN { { \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en } }
    connect \ADDR \mem_w_addr
    connect \DATA \mem_w_data
  end
  process $group_0
    assign \mem_w_addr 2'00
    assign \mem_w_addr \w_addr
    sync init
  end
  process $group_1
    assign \mem_w_en 1'0
    assign \mem_w_en \w_en
    sync init
  end
  process $group_2
    assign \mem_w_data 8'00000000
    assign \mem_w_data \w_data
    sync init
  end
end
```

Driver:
```c++
#include "repro.cc"
#include <iostream>

cxxrtl_design::p_top top;

void tick() {
        top.p_clk.next = value<1>{1u};
        top.step();
        top.p_clk.next = value<1>{0u};
        top.step();
}

int main() {
        top.step();

        top.p_w__en.next = value<1>{1u};
        top.p_w__addr.next = value<2>{0u};
        top.p_w__data.next = value<8>{(uint8_t)'a'};
        tick();

        top.p_w__addr.next = value<2>{1u};
        top.p_w__data.next = value<8>{(uint8_t)'b'};
        tick();

        top.p_w__addr.next = value<2>{2u};
        top.p_w__data.next = value<8>{(uint8_t)'c'};
        tick();

        top.p_w__en.next = value<1>{0u};
        tick();

        std::cout << "mem[0] = " << (char)top.memory_p_mem[0].data[0] << "\n";
        std::cout << "mem[1] = " << (char)top.memory_p_mem[1].data[0] << "\n";
        std::cout << "mem[2] = " << (char)top.memory_p_mem[2].data[0] << "\n";

        return 0;
}
```

## Expected behavior

```
mem[0] = a
mem[1] = b
mem[2] = c
```
Regardless of `write_cxxrtl` optimization level.

## Actual behavior

write_cxxrtl -O2:
```
mem[0] = a
mem[1] = b
mem[2] = c
```

write_cxxrtl -O3:
```
mem[0] =
mem[1] = b
mem[2] = c
```
code_blocks:
module \top
  wire width 2 input 0 \w_addr
  wire width 1 input 1 \w_en
  wire width 8 input 2 \w_data
  wire width 1 input 3 \clk
  wire width 1 input 4 \rst
  memory width 8 size 4 \mem
  cell $meminit $1
    parameter \MEMID "\\mem"
    parameter \ABITS 2'11
    parameter \WIDTH 4'1000
    parameter \WORDS 3'100
    parameter \PRIORITY 1'0
    connect \ADDR 3'000
    connect \DATA 32'00000000000000000000000000000000
  end
  wire width 1 \mem_w_en
  wire width 2 \mem_w_addr
  wire width 8 \mem_w_data
  cell $memwr \wp
    parameter \MEMID "\\mem"
    parameter \ABITS 2'10
    parameter \WIDTH 4'1000
    parameter \CLK_ENABLE 1'1
    parameter \CLK_POLARITY 1'1
    parameter \PRIORITY 1'0
    connect \CLK \clk
    connect \EN { { \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en \mem_w_en } }
    connect \ADDR \mem_w_addr
    connect \DATA \mem_w_data
  end
  process $group_0
    assign \mem_w_addr 2'00
    assign \mem_w_addr \w_addr
    sync init
  end
  process $group_1
    assign \mem_w_en 1'0
    assign \mem_w_en \w_en
    sync init
  end
  process $group_2
    assign \mem_w_data 8'00000000
    assign \mem_w_data \w_data
    sync init
  end
end

Regardless of `write_cxxrtl` optimization level.

## Actual behavior

write_cxxrtl -O2:
